title: nepeta ucranica subsp. parviflora
intro: Nepeta ucranica subsp. parviflora, also known as the lesser catmint, is a subspecies of the Nepeta ucranica plant, a member of the mint family native to Eastern Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it a popular remedy for digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Nepeta ucranica subsp. parviflora include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe digestive issues such as diarrhea and nausea. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine.
